item.title: Doctronic AI Doctor Pilot Backlash
item.description:
• Summary: Doctronic's AI doctor pilot in Utah is facing backlash from the state's medical board and local physicians. Critics raise concerns about safety, ethical implications, and the lack of human oversight in AI-driven medical consultations. The pilot aims to test AI-based primary care, but regulatory hurdles and public skepticism threaten its implementation.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: STAT News

item.title: Biospace Journalism Evolution
item.description:
• Summary: BioSpace discusses the evolution of its journalism in the biopharma industry, particularly during the Trump era. The article highlights changes in reporting style, focus on policy, and the need to adapt to a shifting regulatory and political landscape while maintaining rigorous scientific and business coverage.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: BioSpace

item.title: Medical School Late-Life NP to MD
item.description:
• Summary: This article from STAT News examines the growing trend of nurse practitioners (NPs) returning to medical school later in life to become physicians. It explores the motivations behind this career shift, including professional advancement and expanded scope of practice, as well as the challenges faced by older students. The piece also considers the potential impact on the healthcare workforce and patient care.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: STAT News

item.title: Science Journal Retractions Guest Editor
item.description:
• Summary: This STAT News article highlights the increasing problem of retractions in scientific journals, particularly those linked to guest-edited special issues. It discusses how the guest editor model can lead to lapses in peer review and editorial oversight, resulting in a higher rate of retractions. The piece underscores the broader implications for scientific integrity and trust in published research.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: STAT News
